note: Known Cloudflare Workers in the p31ca tree. verify-worker-inventory.mjs flags any wrangler.toml not in this list as P1 (review needed). Add entries as workers are promoted.

workers.name: geodesic-room
workers.corsPolicy: none — WebSocket only, no CORS headers set
workers.authScheme: none — rooms are public by name; no PII stored
workers.notes: Durable Object holding K₄ tetrahedron vertex positions (4 vertices, float xyz). WebSocket broadcast room. Clients drag vertices; DO is authoritative state. Room IDs are user-supplied; no auth required for POC. P2: add CF Access or Bearer token for private rooms.

workers.name: p31-passkey
workers.corsPolicy: wildcard — intentional, protected by WebAuthn rpId binding
workers.notes: WebAuthn passkey backend. ECDSA/RSA verification via SubtleCrypto. CORS is `*` but WebAuthn is secure regardless: authData.rpIdHash must equal SHA-256(RP_ID='p31ca.org'), and clientDataJSON.origin is verified. An attacker relaying from a different origin would fail rpId hash check. P2 improvement: restrict CORS to p31ca.org and p31ca.pages.dev explicitly.

workers.name: p31-google-bridge
workers.corsPolicy: configurable CORS — credentials for /api/*; set CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN; no * in production
workers.authScheme: Google OAuth2 + session cookie; GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET in wrangler secret
workers.notes: User OAuth: calendar.readonly + OIDC. Tokens in KV. Session cookie p31_gb_sid. Callback and success URL must be same site for Set-Cookie. See docs/integrations/GOOGLE-WORKSPACE.md + p31-google-bridge/README.md.
